reference.title: REPL commands reference
reference.shortdesc: Complete reference for all commands available in the md-validate repl interactive session.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Complete reference for all markdown-validator REPL commands: load, dump, query, get, and eval, with syntax and examples.

row.entry: Load a Markdown file. All subsequent commands operate on this file. You can load a new file at any time to switch context.
Print the loaded file's YAML front matter as a formatted JSON object. Use this to confirm which keys are present and their exact values before writing header rules.
Print the rendered HTML of the loaded file's body. Use this to see the exact DOM structure that body-rule XPath expressions will query.
Run an XPath expression against the loaded file's HTML body. Optional flag: text (default), count, or dom. Returns the result as a list.
Retrieve the value of a front-matter key. Equivalent to dump metadata for a single key.
Evaluate a rule JSON object against the loaded document. The rule must be valid JSON on a single line. Returns PASS or FAIL with the rule name and, on failure, the mitigation message.

section.title: Example session
section.codeblock:
> load docs/tutorial-deploy-app.md Loaded: /abs/path/docs/tutorial-deploy-app.md Metadata keys: ['title', 'ms.topic', 'ms.date', 'author', 'description']
> get ms.topic ms.topic: tutorial
> query /html/body/h1 text Result: ['Tutorial: Deploy a web application']
> query //h2 count Result: [4]
> query //h2 text Result: ['Prerequisites', 'Deploy the app', 'Test the deployment', 'Clean up resources']
> eval {"id":1,"name":"h1-must-start-with-tutorial","type":"body","query":"/html/body/h1","flag":"text","operation":"[:","value":"Tutorial:","mitigation":"H1 must start with 'Tutorial:'"} PASS: rule id=1 name='h1-must-start-with-tutorial'
> quit
